_doc: T2.8 calibration corridors — every band is TUNE data (D-006). Two-sided by construction: the battery fails when a metric leaves its band in EITHER direction, and fails when the metric produced no signal at all (no-output-is-failure). Bands were measured on the T2.7b honest dynamics (ADR-011 kernel) and widened to cover seed variance, not to make tests pass: see each band's note.

canonical._doc: Canonical production autoplay (sim autoplay: 1024 px world, N = 12, 650 turns to year 4500). Fed-era corridors: at this scale the world is far below capacity for the whole horizon.

fedGrowthPerYear.note: The ratified T2.7 band 0.05-0.1 %/yr verbatim; measured 0.00072-0.00076 across seeds after the 800-year founding warm-up.

crudeBirthRatePer1000.note: Pre-modern CBR corridor around the measured 41.5; the historical envelope is ~30-55, the tune sits mid-range.

crudeDeathRatePer1000.note: CDR tracks CBR minus the growth band; measured 40.7.

pyramidChildShare.note: Measured 0.410; pre-modern high-fertility pyramids are 0.35-0.45 under-15.

pyramidElderShare.note: Measured 0.026 with the BandViews elder boundary; pre-modern survival past the elder line is rare.

densityPerArableKm2.note: People per fertility-weighted arable km2 at year 4500; measured 0.30-0.36 across seeds. A Neolithic-to-Bronze world sits far below the ~10-30/km2 of mature agrarian land use - the D-015 verdict input.

migrationGrossPerDecade.note: Fraction of population moving per decade; measured 0.0004 in the fed era (famine flight, the big driver, is absent). Upper edge = the T2.5 rig corridor ceiling.

dev._doc: Dev-world autoplay (256 px, N = 4, 1000 turns): the capacity horizon is reachable, so the Malthus corridors live here.

crashCount.note: At least one >=20% boom-crash must occur (measured: exactly 1 per seed, first at t ~ 590-660); more than 3 in 1000 turns would be the oscillation pathology, not the Malthus cycle.

firstCrashTurn.note: The founding boom must END: capacity ~10.2k (dev) is overshot near t ~ 590-660 across seeds.

crashDepth.note: Measured 0.71-0.83 peak-to-trough; a <0.4 dip is a wobble not a crash, >0.95 is extinction.

postCrashPopulation.note: The survivors must oscillate around capacity (~10.2k measured 10.2-11.6k final), not die out and not re-boom past the peak.

starvationRatePer1000.note: Whole-run starvation deaths per 1000 person-years (measured 0.42-0.44): famine demography must EXIST (zero means the crash was bloodless = dodge regression) and must not dominate base mortality.

migrationGrossPerDecade.note: Measured 0.0013-0.0029; famine flight around the crash lifts the dev world above the fed canonical rate.
